title: Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books (Thomas Austin's edited compilation)
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository representation of University of Michigan CME item CookBk, keyed in 1999 from Oxford University Press's 1964 reprint of Early English Text Society Original Series 91. That reprint derives from Thomas Austin's 1888 edition, an edited compilation of substantial transcriptions from British Library Harley MSS 279 and 4016 and selected extracts from Bodleian Ashmole MS 1439, Laud Misc. 553, and Douce MS 55. Austin credits Faulke Watling with copying and partly press-preparing Harley 279, reports collation against Ashmole 1439 and Douce 55, and thanks F. J. Furnivall for checking the text against both principal manuscripts. A complete 1888 Internet Archive scan, OCR, and metadata are cached with fixity. Current catalog and access review found no public complete facsimile for any of the five manuscripts as of 2026-07-14; the two Bodleian digital objects expose only one selected canvas each, locally cached as IIIF bundles, and neither selected canvas is asserted to overlap Austin's extracts.
review_notes: The primary subject is CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/CookBk.xml. The U-M encoding, 1964 reprint, 1888 edition, Watling working transcript, five manuscripts, catalog descriptions, and digital facsimiles are separate layers.
The intellectual unit is Austin's exact edited compilation, not one homogeneous medieval work and not the complete contents of any source codex.

The 1888 title page prints Ashmole MS 1429, while Austin's foreword and text, the 1964 reprint, U-M description, and current Bodleian record identify Ashmole MS 1439. The former is preserved as an edition-level error or conflict, not a sixth witness.

Austin calls the later Harley 279 contents table leaf 57 and probably eighteenth century; the current British Library catalog identifies f. 44r and seventeenth century. Neither foliation nor date is silently normalized.

The current British Library records expose no Digitised Content for Harley 279 or 4016; Ashmole 1439 has no Digital Images link; Laud Misc. 553 and Douce 55 expose only one selected canvas each. These are dated public-access findings, not proof that institutional, unpublished, or legacy photography does not exist.

The official EETS 2026 publication list offers Original Series 91 as a paperback under ISBN 9780859918497. That currently sold carrier is modeled separately because the list does not identify it as the exact 1964 physical issue or document its printing genealogy.

The U-M item currently applies Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, while the embedded legacy electronic-text notice permits personal, research, and teaching use but forbids redistribution. Both source-layer statements are preserved pending institutional clarification.

The pinned CME LICENSE uses Unlicense wording framed as software. Its application to XML, source data, markup, and later repository-authored material is unresolved and is not broadened into a confident content license.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CookBk
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: DLPSTEXTCLASS/@ID identifies CookBk; the file is 377,154 bytes.
Repository fixity is anchored to both the pinned submodule commit and the exact git blob.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CookBk
entities.description: A 1999 keyboarded electronic transcription of the 1964 EETS reprint. It includes nearly all front matter and the full printed medieval text through p. 117, but omits the Harley 4016 contents on pp. 65-66, glossary, index, and EETS administrative back matter.
notes: The source profile names the keyboarder only as 'Chrisian'; no identity is inferred from that incomplete or erroneous form.
The profile reports a review of five percent of the text and an estimated error rate no greater than one per 20,000 keystrokes.

entities.description: The immediate printed source named by U-M: an Oxford University Press reprint of Austin's EETS edition, with Ashmole 1439 in the source title.

The list establishes present availability, ISBN, format, and price but does not identify this carrier as the exact 1964 physical issue or document its intervening printing history.

entities.label: Thomas Austin, Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books (1888)
entities.description: The first publication of Austin's exact compilation: editorial front matter; Harley 279 on printed pp. 1-64; Harley 4016 on pp. 65-107; nineteen Ashmole sauces on pp. 108-110; twenty-five Laud recipes on pp. 111-114; and twelve Douce recipes on pp. 115-117, followed by glossary and index.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Austin, ed., Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books, Harleian MS. 279 (ab. 1430), & Harl. MS. 4016 (ab. 1450), with Extracts from Ashmole MS. 1429, Laud MS. 553, & Douce MS. 55, Early English Text Society, Original Series 91 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1888).
bibliographic.title: Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 91
bibliographic.pages: xix, 151 plus title, contents, errata, and society matter

notes: The title page's Ashmole 1429 reading conflicts with the edition's foreword and body and with the current catalog's Ashmole 1439 identification.
Austin's printed foreword explicitly describes his selection, Watling's transcript work, and collations.

entities.label: Faulke Watling's working transcript of Harley MS 279
entities.description: A modern handwritten or typescript working witness inferred only from Austin's statement that Watling copied Harley 279 and partly prepared the text for press. It is not a second medieval witness, and its present location and survival are unknown.

date_statements.label: Preparation before publication
date_statements.notes: Austin supplies no exact preparation date.

notes: The manuscript_witness type is used provisionally for an edition-working transcript whose physical form is not documented.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 279
entities.description: An English parchment culinary manuscript containing tables of contents, Potage Dyvers, Vyaunde Leche, Vyaunde Furnez, feast menus, and a later Latin contents table. Austin's pp. 1-64 substantially transcribe its culinary material.

physical_description.extent: 50 folios, plus flyleaves and unfoliated parchment leaves
notes: Current catalog: Gothic cursive script; produced in England; Middle English and Latin.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 4016
entities.description: An English paper cookery book containing a Richard II banquet record at ff. 1r-1v, a John Stafford appointment feast at ff. 1v-2r, and a Boke of kokery of 182 recipes at ff. 2r-28r. Austin prints it on pp. 65-107.

notes: Current catalog: Gothic script; produced in England; Middle English.

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, Ashmole MS 1439
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century English parchment culinary codex. Austin collated parts of the Harley 279 recipe text against it and printed nineteen selected sauce recipes from ff. 36r-37v on pp. 108-110.

notes: Current catalog describes Middle English culinary recipes and menus, with Anglo-Norman and Latin in menu material outside the selected sauce extract.

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, Laud Misc. 553
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English parchment medical and herbal codex. Austin prints twenty-five culinary recipes from two odd leaves, with milestones ff. 5r-6v, on pp. 111-114.

notes: Current catalog describes herbal and medical texts in Middle English; Austin identifies the selected culinary matter as two odd leaves.

entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, Douce MS 55
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century English parchment cookery manuscript. Austin first intended it as a principal text, then substituted the more complete Harley 4016, collated Harley 4016 against Douce, and printed twelve Douce extracts on pp. 115-117.

notes: The current record describes 185 capitula; Austin reports 184 recipes. Parts of ff. 1-32 have been cut away and text is injured.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Austin's 1888 edition
entities.description: A complete 184-page provider PDF of the 1888 volume with OCR and provider metadata. The title page is PDF page 9, the foreword begins at PDF page 13, printed pp. 1-117 occupy PDF pages 27-143, and glossary/index pp. 119-151 occupy PDF pages 145-177.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF, page images, machine-generated OCR, and provider metadata
notes: Internet Archive metadata reports imagecount 188 and a damaged or missing portion on printed p. 73; the carrier is nevertheless a complete scan of the surviving copy rather than a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for Laud Misc. 553
entities.description: A one-canvas digital object derived from a 35mm slide and labeled fol. 7v. It is only a selected view, not a complete codex facsimile, and it is not asserted to overlap Austin's ff. 5r-6v extract.
entities.reproduction_format: One IIIF Presentation canvas, locally bundled at a 1600-pixel request profile

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for Douce MS 55
entities.description: A one-canvas Digital Bodleian object labeled fols. 17v-18r. It is only a selected opening, not a complete codex facsimile, and no overlap with Austin's twelve extracts is asserted.
entities.reproduction_format: One IIIF Presentation canvas, locally bundled at a 1600-pixel request profile

Request expert manuscript photography for the complete present physical object first—bindings or boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, unfoliated leaves, blanks, every folio recto and verso, and separately imaged fragments or edges—then identify Austin's culinary range. Ask for an inventory, omissions, color target, scale, resolution, delivery format, total fee, and written reuse terms.

Request expert manuscript photography for the complete present physical object first—bindings or boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every folio recto and verso, and separately imaged fragments or edges—then identify Austin's ff. 1r-28r text. Ask for an inventory, omissions, color target, scale, resolution, delivery format, total fee, and written reuse terms.

Request a complete physical-object sequence before the target extract: bindings or boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every folio recto and verso, fragments, and separately imaged edges. Identify Austin's ff. 36r-37v sauce extract separately and ask for current foliation, image inventory, omissions, resolution, delivery, price, and written private-use, publication, redistribution, and commercial-reuse terms.

Request a complete physical-object sequence beyond the one selected slide: bindings or boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every folio recto and verso, fragments, and separately imaged edges. Identify Austin's ff. 5r-6v extract separately and ask for current foliation, inventory, omissions, resolution, delivery, price, and written reuse terms.

Request a complete physical-object sequence beyond the one selected opening: bindings or boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, all vi preliminary leaves and 89 manuscript leaves recto and verso, damaged or cut areas, fragments, and separately imaged edges. Identify Austin's twelve extracts separately and ask for a current-foliation concordance, inventory, omissions, resolution, delivery, price, and written reuse terms.
Review of the current record, its Digitised Content field, British Library discovery routes, and targeted exact-shelfmark searches.
notes: This dated negative finding concerns an authorized public complete facsimile, not unpublished or internal photography.

rights.component: The CookBk digital text and materials currently presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The current U-M item states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-specific and is not an affirmative worldwide license.

rights.component: Legacy electronic transcription, markup, and distribution notice embedded in the source XML
rights.basis: The embedded notice permits personal, research, and teaching use but expressly prohibits redistribution. Its continued contractual applicability and relationship to the current No Copyright-United States statement are unclear.
notes: The conflicting current and legacy statements are recorded separately rather than silently resolving one in favor of the other.
Clarification is needed before treating the encoding or markup as openly redistributable outside the existing repository context.

rights.component: Repository serialization, XML/data, and any protectable markup or later modifications
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE contains Unlicense wording but frames its subject as software; this review found no source-specific statement establishing that it governs the XML data, upstream transcription, or later project-authored changes.
notes: This records the visible repository license without broadening it into a definitive license for every textual and metadata layer.

rights.component: Austin's 1888 edition, transcription, editorial frame, notes, glossary, and index
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1888, Internet Archive marks the source item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT, and U-M applies No Copyright-United States to its edition-derived item.

rights.component: The 1964 reprint's carrier-specific typography, production matter, and any new editorial changes
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: U-M's current public-domain statement supports the material it presents but does not separately analyze carrier-specific 1964 typography, production matter, or changes; no complete 1964 copy or item-specific license was reviewed.

rights.component: Carrier-specific current printing, typesetting, cover, production matter, and any undocumented changes
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom, United States, and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The official 2026 list establishes a currently sold paperback but supplies no item-level rights notice, printing date, colophon, or contents comparison. Austin's 1888 editorial content is separately assessed as public domain in the United States, but that assessment is not generalized to modern carrier-specific matter.
notes: Purchase does not itself grant redistribution, publication, or commercial reuse rights in modern carrier-specific material.

rights.component: Internet Archive's digital surrogate, PDF, OCR, and metadata as offered
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and publicly offers the files; no separate item-level Creative Commons license was displayed in the inspected metadata.
notes: Public download and a no-known-copyright assertion do not themselves waive any provider terms applying to platform files or metadata.

rights.basis: The manuscript is dated to the 1430s, so its underlying historical text is far outside any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This does not assess modern photographs, digital files, catalog prose, or supply agreements.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple distinct manuscript culinary collections and discontinuous recipe loci
editorial_practices.representation: Two principal manuscript sections followed by three shorter extract sections
editorial_practices.description: Austin selected and arranged Harley 279, substituted Harley 4016 for an initially intended Douce 55 principal text, then added nineteen Ashmole sauces, twenty-five Laud recipes, and twelve Douce recipes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Parallel recipe witnesses and editorial checking against the principal manuscripts
editorial_practices.representation: Ashmole readings marked A, Harley 4016 checked against Douce, and text checked against both principal Harleian manuscripts
editorial_practices.description: Austin reports using Ashmole 1439 to collate relevant Harley 279 recipes and Douce 55 to collate Harley 4016; he thanks Furnivall for checking the text against both principal manuscripts. The exact division of editorial labor and every adopted reading are not reconstructed here.
editorial_practices.scope: Applicable Harley 279 and Harley 4016 passages and the readings appendix

editorial_practices.source_feature: The full 1964 printed volume, including front matter, a Harley 4016 contents list, glossary, index, and society matter
editorial_practices.representation: Nearly all front matter and the medieval text through p. 117; Harley 4016 contents pp. 65-66 and all glossary, index, and society back matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic-text declaration explicitly records the retained and omitted matter. The omitted Harley 4016 contents are noted as editorial rather than manuscript material.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed and manuscript-derived heads, supplied heads, deleted heads, notes, and folio milestones
editorial_practices.representation: DIV heads, NOTE elements, MILESTONE markers, editorial brackets, corrections, gaps, and occasional supplied or deleted headings
editorial_practices.description: The profile says footnotes were represented variously as notes, milestones, corrections, or gaps; Austin-supplied words remain bracketed; some heads were added or deleted; and the Harley 279 menu divisions are Austin's imposed structure.
notes: A fol. 14 milestone was inserted speculatively and should not be treated as direct witness evidence without image comparison.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed superscripts, line- and page-break hyphenation, and typographic details
editorial_practices.representation: Joined break-spanning words; superscripts mostly not preserved; Austin-supplied matter bracketed
editorial_practices.description: The profile documents normalized electronic representation of break-spanning words and loss of most superscript distinction. This is an encoding-layer practice, not a claim about manuscript abbreviation expansion.

evidence.citation: Pinned CME source XML for CookBk
evidence.summary: Supplies the repository artifact, U-M source and encoding profile, 1964 bibliographic source, legacy rights notice, title and contents, transcription, folio milestones, and editorial declarations.

evidence.citation: Local computational and structural inspection of CookBk.xml
evidence.summary: Verified file fixity and byte size; ten DIV1, twenty-one DIV2, eleven DIV3, 538 P, 875 ITEM, fifty-three LIST, eight L, and 137 PB elements; recipe counts of 153, 64, 41, 182, 19, 25, and 12; menu structure; languages; and recorded folio milestones.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.summary: Contains Unlicense wording framed as applying to software. It does not expressly resolve scope for XML data, markup, upstream transcription, or later repository-authored material.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English, CookBk item
evidence.summary: Confirms item identity, Austin editorship, Oxford University Press 1964 source, public table of contents, stable identifier, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States status.

evidence.citation: RightsStatements.org, No Copyright-United States 1.0
evidence.summary: Defines the statement as a jurisdiction-specific indication that the item is believed free of copyright restrictions in the United States, not a worldwide license.

evidence.citation: Early English Text Society, List of Publications 2026
evidence.summary: The official current list offers 'Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books,' ed. T. Austin, as EETS Original Series 91, paperback, ISBN 9780859918497, priced at £25 and $45. Its general availability note says many backlist titles are supplied by print on demand; the OS 91 line does not identify this carrier as the exact 1964 physical issue.

evidence.citation: Thomas Austin, ed., Two Fifteenth-Century Cookery-Books (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1888), complete Internet Archive scan

evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for title-page wording, exact selection boundary, recipe counts, Watling and Furnivall responsibilities, collations, folio milestones, publication details, and complete printed contents.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of the cached Austin 1888 PDF

evidence.citation: MARCO, Bodleian Libraries, MS. Douce 55
evidence.summary: Supplies the vi + 89-leaf extent, mid-fifteenth-century English origin and parchment support, 185-capitulum cookery description, incipit, Austin 1888 collation reference, and note that cuts to ff. 1-32 injure text.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for MS. Laud Misc. 553 selected fol. 7v
evidence.summary: Exposes exactly one canvas labeled fol. 7v, item metadata, photograph credit to Bodleian Libraries, and CC BY-NC 4.0 licensing.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for MS. Douce 55 selected fols. 17v-18r
evidence.summary: Exposes exactly one canvas labeled fols. 17v-18r, item metadata, photograph credit to Bodleian Libraries, and CC BY-NC 4.0 licensing.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and inventory audit of the Laud Misc. 553 selected IIIF bundle
evidence.summary: Verified a valid ZIP with the full one-canvas provider selection and one 1,076,134-byte image; physical-manuscript coverage is partial and does not cover Austin's ff. 5r-6v target.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and inventory audit of the Douce MS 55 selected IIIF bundle
evidence.summary: Verified a valid ZIP with the full one-canvas provider selection and one 520,017-byte image; physical-manuscript coverage is partial and no overlap with Austin's extracts is asserted.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of Use
evidence.summary: Explains item-specific license checking, attribution, common CC BY-NC use of images, and the need to seek permission for commercial reuse.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services
evidence.summary: Describes fee-based ordering by shelfmark, title, and folio side and expert photography for manuscripts rather than ordinary scanning of originals.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services Terms and Conditions
evidence.summary: Current January 2026 terms describe preservation review, fees, delivery conditions, and the requirement for written permission to reproduce supplied images.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries special-collections imaging, remote copying, and ordering guidance
evidence.summary: Provides routes to request images for material not fully available on Digital Bodleian, mediated reference scans, and onsite Special Collections orders; fees and actual supply terms depend on the request.

evidence.citation: Targeted public-facsimile audit for Harley MSS 279 and 4016, Ashmole MS 1439, Laud Misc. 553, and Douce MS 55
evidence.summary: Exact-shelfmark searches and current institutional discovery routes found no authorized public complete manuscript facsimile on 2026-07-14. The only verified Bodleian digital objects contain one selected canvas each for Laud and Douce; the British Library records expose no Digitised Content, and Ashmole exposes no Digital Images link.
notes: This is a dated negative public-access finding, not evidence that no unpublished, internal, paid, or legacy photography exists.

evidence.citation: Combined current institutional catalog evidence for the five source manuscripts
evidence.summary: Grouping evidence used only by the paired metadata manifest; the individual catalog evidence records above preserve source-specific claims.

open_questions.question: Can the holding institutions supply or identify complete physical-object facsimiles for all five source manuscripts?
next_steps: Ask each institution about unlinked complete color photography, legacy microfilm, or internal digital surrogates.
Request the complete present physical objects rather than only Austin's excerpt loci, with an explicit image inventory and omissions statement.
Obtain written terms for private retention, computational analysis, repository redistribution, scholarly publication, and commercial use separately.

open_questions.question: How do Austin's source milestones map to current foliation, especially Harley 279 leaf 57 versus current f. 44r and the unresolved opening leaf of the Douce extract?
next_steps: Inspect complete manuscript sequences and current foliation directly.
Build an edition-page to historical-foliation to current-foliation concordance without filling gaps from inference.
Resolve Austin's Douce count of 184 recipes against the current catalog's 185 capitula.

open_questions.question: Does Watling's working transcript survive, and what were the exact transcription, press-preparation, and collation contributions of Watling, Furnivall, and Austin?
next_steps: Search EETS archives and Austin, Furnivall, and Watling papers or correspondence.
Compare any surviving transcript and proofs against the manuscripts and 1888 print.

open_questions.question: What textual-version relationships among the five manuscript streams can be established beyond Austin's reported Ashmole and Douce collations?
next_steps: Consult modern recipe concordances and textual scholarship.
Collate complete witness images and encode only secure version relations without assuming physical copying direction.

open_questions.question: How do U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement, the embedded legacy no-redistribution notice, and the pinned CME Unlicense apply to the electronic transcription, XML markup, repository serialization, and downstream publication?
next_steps: Ask U-M whether the current item statement supersedes the legacy redistribution restriction and which layers it covers.
Ask Kyvero Press whether the Unlicense is intended to cover XML/data and upstream transcription as well as software.
Do not generalize either statement to modern manuscript photography or future repository-authored matter.

open_questions.question: What caused the 1888 title page to print Ashmole 1429 when the edition's foreword and text and current catalog identify Ashmole 1439?
next_steps: Inspect EETS proofs, errata, Austin's papers, and later reprint editorial records.
Retain 1429 only as literal title-page evidence unless an independent witness is documented.
